7:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
8:["$","$L21",null,{"children":["$","$22",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@23"}]}]
9:["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Speakers win for: long listening sessions where headphone fatigue accumulates, home offices where you work alone and ambient sound is controlled, video calls with multiple people in the room, and background music you want to hear naturally."}}]
a:["$","p","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Most home office workers benefit from both. Speakers for background music and ambient sound during focus work; headphones for calls and deep focus sessions requiring silence."}}]
b:["$","h2","4",{"id":"what-to-look-for-in-desk-speakers","children":"What to Look For in Desk Speakers"}]
c:["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Form factor</strong>: Bookshelf speakers (two separate units) produce better stereo separation. Desktop speakers (often a 2.1 system with a subwoofer) add bass but take more desk space. Compact stereo speakers split the difference."}}]
d:["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Connectivity</strong>: USB audio is the simplest connection — powered by the computer, no separate power supply. Bluetooth adds wireless flexibility. 3.5mm aux is universal. The best desk speakers offer multiple options."}}]
e:["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Volume at desk distance</strong>: Desk speakers operate at 1–2 metres from your ears. You need precise volume control at low listening levels — a speaker that sounds good loud but has a scratchy or imprecise volume pot at low levels is frustrating for all-day use."}}]
f:["$","p","8",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Footprint</strong>: Desk real estate is limited. Measure before buying. A speaker wider than 15 cm per side is significant desk furniture."}}]
10:["$","h2","9",{"id":"best-desk-speakers-in-2026","children":"Best Desk Speakers in 2026"}]
11:["$","h3","10",{"id":"best-overall-audioengine-a2-250","children":"Best Overall: Audioengine A2+ (~£250)"}]
12:["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The definitive compact desktop speaker. Outstanding sound for the size, USB audio (no separate DAC required), and a built-in amplifier. Small enough to sit beside most monitors. The A2+ sounds genuinely good at desk listening distances — which is rarer than it should be at any price."}}]
13:["$","h3","12",{"id":"best-budget-edifier-r1280db-90","children":"Best Budget: Edifier R1280DB (~£90)"}]
14:["$","p","13",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Bluetooth, optical, and RCA inputs. Remote control. Subwoofer output. Excellent clarity for the price and a warm, non-fatiguing sound that works well for background music during work. The best entry-level desk speaker."}}]
15:["$","h3","14",{"id":"best-for-small-desks-kanto-yu2-140","children":"Best for Small Desks: Kanto YU2 (~£140)"}]
16:["$","p","15",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Genuinely compact (less than 12 cm wide per speaker) with phono preamp, USB, Bluetooth, and outstanding sound for the size. The choice when desk space is genuinely limited."}}]
17:["$","h3","16",{"id":"best-premium-kef-lsx-ii-1-000","children":"Best Premium: KEF LSX II (~£1,000)"}]
18:["$","p","17",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Wireless, app-controlled, Ethernet connectivity, and sound that competes with much larger speakers. A luxury choice, but if you work with audio professionally or care deeply about sound quality during the workday, the LSX II is worth it."}}]
19:["$","h2","18",{"id":"speaker-placement-on-a-desk","children":"Speaker Placement on a Desk"}]
1a:["$","p","19",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For accurate stereo imaging, speakers should form an equilateral triangle with your listening position — each speaker the same distance from you as they are from each other."}}]
1b:["$","p","20",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"On a desk, this typically means: speakers placed at the outer edges of the desk surface, angled slightly inward toward your seated position. Tweeters at ear level. Keep speakers away from walls (at least 20 cm from the back wall) to prevent bass buildup."}}]
1c:["$","p","21",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For focus sessions where speakers would be distracting, read our comparison of <a href=\"/blog/headphones-vs-earbuds-for-work\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">headphones vs earbuds for work</a>."}}]
